scope.goal: Tie the mapped auth shadow/reset handlers to the generic selector-table runtime dispatcher, and record the remaining JS5/client-script export blocker.

runtime_dispatcher.caller_role: Drains 0x10-byte client/root queue records and invokes the generic selector-table dispatcher.

selector_source.interpretation: Reads a 16-bit selector from the queued stream/descriptor attached to the parser/session object.

handler_invocation.meaning: Second handler argument is the queued parser/session object.
First handler argument is the dispatcher/root context.

auth_selector_entries.role: one-shot auth shadow export triple drain
auth_selector_entries.runtime_dispatch: When the queued descriptor selector is 0x10E, 0x14008CAD0 computes slot 0x140E5F4E0 and calls handler 0x1401F5150.
handler_effects: Reads auth_controller+0x1BC and pushes it as the latched non-selector-1 route status.
Reads auth_controller+0x1C4 and pushes it as the latched +0x178 export value.
Reads auth_controller+0x1C0 and pushes it as the latched +0x174 sentinel/index value.
Clears +0x1BC to -2, +0x1C4 to -1, and +0x1C0 to -1 after the push.

auth_selector_entries.runtime_dispatch: When the queued descriptor selector is 0x798, 0x14008CAD0 computes slot 0x140E65D80 and calls handler 0x1401F5100.
handler_effects: Runs the reset path only when auth_controller+0x10 is zero.
Marks [source_object+0x78]+0x234 as 1 when the child pointer exists.

relationship_to_snapshot_path.consumer: Selector 0x10E drains this latched triple through the generic runtime dispatcher and then clears it.
relationship_to_snapshot_path.interpretation: The client has live auth status/export fields and separate one-shot shadow fields. The shadow fields are consumed by a selector-table handler, not by fake UI/update-server literal text.

js5_clientscript_blocker.current_state: No JS5 export manifest or cache-derived clientscript export is present in the local artifacts tree, so script-side call-site naming is not yet evidence-backed.
checked_for: local JS5/cache export artifacts under artifacts/external-targets/rs2client-947
js5_clientscript_blocker.expected_output: Map the scripts or queued descriptor producers that emit 0x10E and 0x798, then correlate them with the direct auth-controller dword export opcodes.

opennxt_947_impact: OpenNXT compatibility work should model selector 0x10E as a one-shot auth result triple drain: replaying the same latched values after consumption should not work unless the snapshot path runs again.
OpenNXT harnesses should model selector 0x798 as an idle reset path for live route status/source state, separate from the 0x10E shadow latch.
The queued selector value read by 0x14008CB3D is now the next concrete bridge between clientscript/cache data and native auth behavior.
The auth dword exports remain native state values, not endpoint strings or placeholder update/auth literals.
next_targets: Produce or locate the JS5/cache export manifest required by js5-opcode-probe.
Trace the producer for the queued descriptor selector consumed at 0x14008CB3D.
Map the unresolved producer of auth_controller+0x178, which feeds direct opcode 0x875 and the middle value of selector 0x10E.
